commit.message:
Fix cipher suite typo: POLY1205 -> POLY1305 in TLS docs (#5879)

`ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1205` was listed as a valid cipher suite in
three SSL/TLS documentation pages — the correct name is
`ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305`. Copying the incorrect value results in
`SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list:no cipher match` errors.

## Changes

- **`solutions/observability/apm/apm-server/ssl-tls-input-settings.md`**
— corrected cipher name
-
**`solutions/observability/apm/apm-server/ssl-tls-output-settings.md`**
— corrected cipher name
- **`reference/fleet/elastic-agent-ssl-configuration.md`** — corrected
cipher name

All other cipher suite names in these files were verified against the Go
`crypto/tls` source and are correct.

Part of https://github.com/elastic/apm-server/issues/20876

commit.message:
ci: add smoke test workflow with branch protection

Runs bash test/smoke.sh on every PR targeting main and on every push to
main. Requires all 23 smoke tests to pass before a merge is allowed.

Security hardening for a public repository:
- pull_request trigger (not pull_request_target): fork PRs run in a
  restricted context with no secrets and a read-only GITHUB_TOKEN,
  preventing privilege-escalation via workflow modification.
- permissions: {} at workflow level, contents: read at job level —
  no write access granted at any scope.
- actions/checkout pinned to commit SHA (v4.2.2) to prevent
  tag-mutable supply chain attacks.
- timeout-minutes: 15 to prevent runaway jobs.
- concurrency group cancels stale in-progress runs on new pushes.

To enforce this as a merge gate: in repo Settings → Branches, add a
branch protection rule for main requiring the 'Smoke tests' status
check to pass before merging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add complete .NET 10 Shaiya MMORPG solution (API + Blazor)

- Sh.Api: ASP.NET Core Web API with JWT auth, 4 EF Core DbContexts (PS_UserData, PS_GameData, PS_GameLog, PS_WebSite), rate limiting, CORS, CSRF, security headers, API key middleware for launcher routes

- Services: AuthService (BCrypt+JWT), TurnstileService (Cloudflare), ShopService, RouletteService, RedeemCodeService, BattlePassService, VoteService, ServerManager
- Middleware: SecurityHeadersMiddleware, ApiKeyMiddleware
- Sh.Web: Blazor Server with cookie auth, ApiService, AuthStateProvider
- Pages: Home, PvP Ranking, Server Info, Download, ToS, Auth (Login/Register/ForgotPassword/ResetPassword), Dashboard, Shop, Roulette, Redeem, Vote, BattlePass, Donate, Admin panel

feat(acceleration): BackendRegistry O(n²) → O(k) backend selection index (#4620)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): BackendRegistry O(n²) backend selection index optimization (Issue #236)

- Add RegisteredBackend struct to compute_backend.h with pre-cast typed
  interface pointers (base, vectorPtr, graphPtr, geoPtr, matrixPtr)
- Add typeIndex_ (unordered_map<BackendType, RegisteredBackend>) to BackendRegistry
- registerBackend(): populate typeIndex_ with one dynamic_cast per interface
  at registration time — never in the hot query path
- selectTyped<T>(): now O(|kFallbackOrder|) using map lookup instead of
  O(|kFallbackOrder| × |backends|) nested scan with dynamic_cast per iteration
- getBackend(): O(1) hash-map lookup via typeIndex_
- getBestVectorBackend/GraphBackend/GeoBackend/MatrixBackend(): use typed
  pointers from typeIndex_ directly — zero dynamic_cast calls
- selectBackendFor(): keeps original linear scan (backward compatible for
  multi-backend-per-type correctness with generic IComputeBackend* return)
- shutdownAll(): clears typeIndex_ alongside backends_
- Add 7 new type-index correctness tests to test_backend_selection_matrix.cpp
- Mark all 3 implementation notes as [x] in FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md files
- Add Phase 5 ROADMAP entry for the optimization

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves indented code blocks as fenced code blocks (#1134)

* fix: Markdown.ToMd preserves indented code blocks as fenced code blocks

Indented code blocks (parsed with fence = None) were serialised by
Markdown.ToMd as bare text without any code block markers. When the
output was re-parsed, the content was interpreted as a Paragraph, not
a CodeBlock — breaking the round-trip.

Fix: use triple-backtick fences for code blocks that originally had no
fence, ensuring the serialised form is always a valid fenced code block.
Fenced code blocks round-trip correctly and render identically to
indented code blocks in all Markdown renderers.

Added a round-trip test to cover the case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

When Markdown.ToMd serialises a parsed MarkdownDocument back to Markdown
text, YamlFrontmatter paragraphs were silently dropped. Documents
containing a YAML front-matter block (--- ... ---) would lose that block
on round-trip.

Fix: emit the --- delimiters and frontmatter lines in formatParagraph so
round-trips are lossless.

Add two new tests: one with populated frontmatter and one with an empty
block.

commit.message:
feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service — KG-backed urgency + resolution (ENG-03) (#143)

* feat(engagement): foreshadowing tracker service (ENG-03)

Implement ForeshadowingTracker — a backend service that queries KG entities
of type 'foreshadowing' and provides urgency-sorted listing + resolution
capability.

Key decisions:
- Pure SQLite queries (INV-4: Memory-First, zero LLM calls)
- resolve() is a structured data operation (attributes_json flag toggle),
  NOT a Skill invocation (INV-6 compliant)
- Deterministic clock injection (P4) — no Date.now() in logic
- 30s TTL cache consistent with storyStatusService
- Graceful degradation: returns [] until kg_entities CHECK constraint is
  extended to include 'foreshadowing' type (migration 0013 gap)
- Imports DbLike/DbStatement from storyStatusService (shared DB abstraction)

Test coverage: 37 tests using in-memory SQLite (real SQL queries, not mocks):
- listActive: empty project, single/multiple items, urgency sort, resolved
  exclusion, project isolation, type filtering
- resolve: success, non-existent, wrong project, already-resolved, attribute
  preservation, cache auto-invalidation
- openDays/urgency: exact days, partial days, today
- firstChapterHint: present, absent, non-string, empty attributes
- Cache: TTL (30s), boundary (29999ms vs 30000ms), invalidation, per-project
- Dispose: listActive throws, resolve throws, cache cleared

Closes #142

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engagement): align foreshadowing tracker with KG schema + spec urgency model

R1 audit findings addressed:
- ISO timestamp parsing: created_at is TEXT in production (kgCoreService
  writes new Date().toISOString()), now parsed via new Date(str).getTime()
- resolve() writes ISO string for updated_at (matches KG convention)
- urgency field: clamped to [0, 1] range via min(1.0, openDays / 30),
  with @deviation JSDoc documenting chapter-based spec vs time-based impl
- Test schema: created_at/updated_at changed from INTEGER to TEXT to match
  production kg_entities schema
- URGENCY_DECAY_DAYS = 30 ≈ 10 chapters at ~3 days/chapter writing pace

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
